toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""P21 visual verification: double-click opens Lightbox, single-click selects.

What this proves:
  1. Single-click image card -> DetailPanel visible, NO Lightbox dialog
  2. Double-click image card -> Lightbox full-screen
  3. Close Lightbox -> DetailPanel still showing the image
  4. List-view double-click image row -> Lightbox full-screen
  5. Double-click document card -> NO Lightbox, DetailPanel shows document
  6. Phone viewport double-click image -> Lightbox full-screen

Setup: register a user via API, create an org, upload 1 image + 1 document.
Log in via the UI, drive click/dblclick interactions, take screenshots.

Outputs:
  p21-grid-click-image.png           desktop, after single-click image
  p21-grid-dblclick-image.png        desktop, after dblclick image
  p21-grid-dblclick-then-close.png   desktop, after closing the Lightbox
  p21-list-dblclick-image.png        desktop, list view, dblclick image row
  p21-grid-dblclick-document.png     desktop, dblclick document (no Lightbox)
  p21-phone-dblclick-image.png       phone viewport, dblclick image
  p21-report.json                    machine-readable summary
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import json
import re
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
import urllib.request
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright, Page

OUT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
WEB_URL = "http://localhost:5173/"
API_URL = "http://localhost:3000"

RUN_ID = int(time.time())
TEST_EMAIL = f"p21-verify-{RUN_ID}@example.com"
TEST_PASSWORD = "TestPass1234"
TEST_NAME = "P21 Verifier"
TEST_ORG = f"P21 Org {RUN_ID}"

DEV_COOKIE = "dam_session"

# 1x1 transparent PNG.
TINY_PNG_B64 = (
    "iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAQAAAC1HAwCAAAAC0lEQVR42mNkYAAAAAYAAjCB0C8AAAAASUVORK5CYII="
)
TINY_PNG_BYTES = base64.b64decode(TINY_PNG_B64)

# Plain-text "document" — any non-empty bytes work; the API only checks size.
TINY_DOC_BYTES = b"%PDF-1.4\n% P21 verify fixture\n"


def api_post(path: str, body: dict, cookies: dict | None = None) -> tuple[int, dict, dict]:
    data = json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{API_URL}{path}",
        data=data,
        method="POST",
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )
    if cookies:
        cookie_str = "; ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in cookies.items())
        req.add_header("Cookie", cookie_str)
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
            raw_cookies = resp.getheader("Set-Cookie") or ""
            return resp.status, json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8") or "{}"), _parse_cookies(raw_cookies)
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        return e.code, json.loads(e.read().decode("utf-8") or "{}"), {}


def _parse_cookies(set_cookie_header: str) -> dict:
    cookies = {}
    if not set_cookie_header:
        return cookies
    for piece in set_cookie_header.split(","):
        first = piece.strip().split(";", 1)[0]
        if "=" in first:
            k, v = first.split("=", 1)
            cookies[k.strip()] = v.strip()
    return cookies


def _put_to_s3(url: str, body: bytes, content_type: str) -> None:
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        url,
        data=body,
        method="PUT",
        headers={
            "Content-Type": content_type,
            "Content-Length": str(len(body)),
        },
    )
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
        if resp.status not in (200, 204):
            raise RuntimeError(f"S3 PUT failed: {resp.status}")


def setup_test_state() -> tuple[dict, str, str, str]:
    """Create a user with an org and 1 image + 1 document asset.
    Returns (cookies, org_id, image_name, doc_name)."""
    print(f"[setup] Registering {TEST_EMAIL}...")
    status, _, cookies = api_post("/api/v1/auth/register", {
        "email": TEST_EMAIL,
        "password": TEST_PASSWORD,
        "displayName": TEST_NAME,
    })
    if status not in (200, 201):
        raise RuntimeError(f"register failed: {status}")
    print(f"        -> {status}, cookies: {list(cookies.keys())}")

    session = cookies.get(DEV_COOKIE)
    if not session:
        raise RuntimeError(f"no {DEV_COOKIE} cookie from register: {cookies}")

    print(f"[setup] Creating org {TEST_ORG!r}...")
    status, body, _ = api_post("/api/v1/orgs", {"name": TEST_ORG}, cookies)
    if status != 200:
        raise RuntimeError(f"createOrg failed: {status} {body}")
    org_id = body["data"]["org"]["id"]
    print(f"        -> org {org_id}")

    image_name = f"p21-image-{RUN_ID}.png"
    doc_name = f"p21-doc-{RUN_ID}.pdf"

    for filename, mime, atype, fmt, body_bytes in [
        (image_name, "image/png", "image", "PNG", TINY_PNG_BYTES),
        (doc_name, "application/pdf", "document", "PDF", TINY_DOC_BYTES),
    ]:
        status, body, _ = api_post(
            f"/api/v1/orgs/{org_id}/uploads",
            {
                "filename": filename,
                "mimeType": mime,
                "size": len(body_bytes),
                "type": atype,
                "format": fmt,
            },
            cookies,
        )
        if status != 200:
            raise RuntimeError(f"initiate upload {filename} failed: {status} {body}")
        asset_id = body["data"]["assetId"]
        upload_url = body["data"]["uploadUrl"]

        _put_to_s3(upload_url, body_bytes, mime)

        status, body, _ = api_post(
            f"/api/v1/orgs/{org_id}/assets/{asset_id}/finalize",
            {},
            cookies,
        )
        if status != 200:
            raise RuntimeError(f"finalize {filename} failed: {status} {body}")

    print(f"        -> uploaded 1 image + 1 document")
    return cookies, org_id, image_name, doc_name


def login_via_ui(page: Page, email: str, password: str) -> None:
    """Open the app and log in. Returns after the search input appears."""
    page.goto(WEB_URL, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
    page.locator("h1", has_text="An archive, organized.").wait_for(
        state="visible", timeout=15_000
    )
    page.locator("#login-email").fill(email)
    page.locator("#login-password").fill(password)
    page.locator("button[type='submit']").click()
    # Wait for the post-login UI to appear.
    page.locator("input[type='search']").wait_for(state="visible", timeout=15_000)
    # Hard reload so StoreProvider hydrates with the new session.
    page.reload(wait_until="domcontentloaded")
    page.wait_for_timeout(2000)


def main() -> int:
    report: dict = {
        "run_id": RUN_ID,
        "email": TEST_EMAIL,
        "results": [],
        "passed": False,
        "errors": [],
    }

    cookies, org_id, image_name, doc_name = setup_test_state()

    def record(name: str, passed: bool, details: str) -> None:
        report["results"].append({"name": name, "passed": passed, "details": details})
        print(f"  {'✓' if passed else '✗'} {name}: {details}")

    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True)

        def fulfill_s3_get(route):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
            req = route.request
            if "localhost:9000" in req.url and req.method == "GET":
                route.fulfill(
                    status=200,
                    content_type="image/png",
                    body=base64.b64decode(TINY_PNG_B64),
                )
            else:
                route.continue_()

        # Desktop context
        ctx = browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1440, "height": 900})
        ctx.route("**/*", fulfill_s3_get)
        page = ctx.new_page()

        # Login
        print(f"[setup] Logging in as {TEST_EMAIL}...")
        login_via_ui(page, TEST_EMAIL, TEST_PASSWORD)

        # Case 1: single-click image card -> DetailPanel visible, no Lightbox.
        print("\n[1/6] Single-click image card (must NOT open Lightbox)...")
        img_card = page.get_by_role("button", name=re.compile(re.escape(image_name))).first
        img_card.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10_000)
        img_card.click()
        page.wait_for_timeout(200)
        lightbox_count = page.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        detail_rename = page.locator('[title="点击重命名"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-grid-click-image",
            lightbox_count == 0 and detail_rename > 0,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}, detail_rename_count={detail_rename}",
        )
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-grid-click-image.png"), full_page=True)

        # Case 2: double-click image card -> Lightbox full-screen.
        print("\n[2/6] Double-click image card (opens Lightbox)...")
        # The dblclick must be on the same card. Use a fresh locator.
        img_card = page.get_by_role("button", name=re.compile(re.escape(image_name))).first
        img_card.dblclick()
        page.wait_for_timeout(300)
        lightbox_count = page.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-grid-dblclick-image",
            lightbox_count == 1,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}",
        )
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-grid-dblclick-image.png"), full_page=True)

        # Case 3: close the Lightbox -> DetailPanel still visible with the image.
        print("\n[3/6] Close Lightbox (DetailPanel still visible)...")
        # The floating ✕ fades out after 2s idle (cinema mode). Use a
        # direct DOM .click() call to bypass Playwright's visibility
        # check (cinema mode keeps the element in the DOM but at 0
        # opacity, which makes Playwright consider it "not visible").
        page.evaluate(
            "document.querySelector('[data-testid=\"lightbox-floating-close\"]').click()"
        )
        page.wait_for_timeout(800)
        lightbox_count = page.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        detail_rename = page.locator('[title="点击重命名"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-grid-dblclick-then-close",
            lightbox_count == 0 and detail_rename > 0,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}, detail_rename_count={detail_rename}",
        )
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-grid-dblclick-then-close.png"), full_page=True)

        # Case 4: list view + dblclick image row -> Lightbox full-screen.
        print("\n[4/6] List view double-click image row (opens Lightbox)...")
        # Switch to list view by pressing '2' (keyboard shortcut).
        page.keyboard.press("2")
        page.wait_for_timeout(400)
        # The select button has aria-label "选择 <filename>".
        list_select = page.get_by_role(
            "button", name=re.compile(r"选择 " + re.escape(image_name))
        ).first
        list_select.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10_000)
        list_select.dblclick()
        page.wait_for_timeout(300)
        lightbox_count = page.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-list-dblclick-image",
            lightbox_count == 1,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}",
        )
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-list-dblclick-image.png"), full_page=True)
        # Close lightbox before next case (bypass visibility check via JS).
        page.evaluate(
            "document.querySelector('[data-testid=\"lightbox-floating-close\"]').click()"
        )
        page.wait_for_timeout(400)
        # Switch back to grid view.
        page.keyboard.press("1")
        page.wait_for_timeout(400)

        # Case 5: dblclick document card -> no Lightbox, DetailPanel shows document.
        print("\n[5/6] Double-click document card (must NOT open Lightbox)...")
        doc_card = page.get_by_role("button", name=re.compile(re.escape(doc_name))).first
        doc_card.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10_000)
        doc_card.dblclick()
        page.wait_for_timeout(200)
        lightbox_count = page.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        detail_rename = page.locator('[title="点击重命名"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-grid-dblclick-document",
            lightbox_count == 0 and detail_rename > 0,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}, detail_rename_count={detail_rename}",
        )
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-grid-dblclick-document.png"), full_page=True)

        ctx.close()

        # Case 6: phone viewport dblclick image -> Lightbox.
        print("\n[6/6] Phone viewport double-click image (opens Lightbox)...")
        ctx_phone = browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 480, "height": 800})
        ctx_phone.route("**/*", fulfill_s3_get)
        page_p = ctx_phone.new_page()
        login_via_ui(page_p, TEST_EMAIL, TEST_PASSWORD)
        # Default view on phone is grid; switch to list view (the natural
        # phone layout, where StackedCardList renders rows) by pressing
        # "2" — the same keyboard shortcut used on desktop.
        page_p.keyboard.press("2")
        page_p.wait_for_timeout(400)
        # On phone, the StackedCardList renders rows with role="listitem"
        # and onDoubleClick attached to the row. The select button
        # (role=button, aria-label "选择 <filename>") is a child. We
        # dblclick on the row's text div (the element holding the asset
        # name + meta) — NOT the row's center (which can hit the select
        # button) and NOT the row itself (same issue, the row's center
        # is the select button). The text div is a flex sibling of the
        # select button, so clicking it bubbles up to the row without
        # opening the BottomSheet. The first click hits the text div
        # (no handler), the second click hits the same div (no handler),
        # and the synthesized dblclick bubbles to the row's onDoubleClick
        # → onOpen → OPEN_LIGHTBOX.
        text_p = page_p.locator(
            f'[role="listitem"]:has-text("{image_name}") [class*="name"]'
        ).first
        if text_p.count() == 0:
            # Fallback to the .text class if name is not found.
            text_p = page_p.locator(
                f'[role="listitem"]:has-text("{image_name}") [class*="text"]'
            ).first
        if text_p.count() == 0:
            # Last-resort: dblclick on the row itself.
            text_p = page_p.locator(
                f'[role="listitem"]:has-text("{image_name}")'
            ).first
        text_p.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10_000)
        # The StackedCardList row's `<button.selectButton>` is
        # `position: absolute; inset: 0; z-index: 1` — it covers the
        # whole row so any single-click on the row hits it. We force
        # the dblclick to bypass Playwright's "topmost element" check
        # and dispatch the synthetic clicks at the text div's center.
        # The first click hits the select button (SELECT_ASSET,
        # BottomSheet opens); the second click hits the BottomSheet
        # backdrop (which closes the BottomSheet on click); the
        # synthesized dblclick event fires on the select button and
        # bubbles to the row's onDoubleClick → onOpen →
        # OPEN_LIGHTBOX.
        text_p.dblclick(force=True)
        page_p.wait_for_timeout(300)
        lightbox_count = page_p.locator('[data-testid="lightbox"]').count()
        record(
            "p21-phone-dblclick-image",
            lightbox_count == 1,
            f"lightbox_count={lightbox_count}",
        )
        page_p.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "p21-phone-dblclick-image.png"), full_page=True)
        ctx_phone.close()

        browser.close()

    total = len(report["results"])
    passed = sum(1 for r in report["results"] if r["passed"])
    report["total"] = total
    report["passed_count"] = passed
    report["passed"] = passed == total

    (OUT / "p21-report.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(report, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    print("=" * 80)
    print(f"Result: {passed}/{total} pass")
    return 0 if report["passed"] else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

input.content:
# Plan 21 — Visual Verification Summary

**Result:** 6/6 pass
**Date:** 2026-06-08
**Branch:** `feat/web-double-click-lightbox`

## What this proves

| # | Case | Screenshot | Pass |
|---|------|------------|------|
| 1 | Single-click image card → DetailPanel visible, no Lightbox | `p21-grid-click-image.png` | ✅ |
| 2 | Double-click image card → Lightbox full-screen | `p21-grid-dblclick-image.png` | ✅ |
| 3 | Close Lightbox → DetailPanel still visible with the image | `p21-grid-dblclick-then-close.png` | ✅ |
| 4 | List view double-click image row → Lightbox full-screen | `p21-list-dblclick-image.png` | ✅ |
| 5 | Double-click document card → no Lightbox, DetailPanel shows document | `p21-grid-dblclick-document.png` | ✅ |
| 6 | Phone viewport double-click image → Lightbox full-screen | `p21-phone-dblclick-image.png` | ✅ |

## Setup

- Real dev environment: API on `localhost:3000`, web on `localhost:5173`, MinIO on `localhost:9000`
- Test fixtures created via real API endpoints (`POST /auth/register`, `/orgs`, `/uploads` draft + finalize for 1 image + 1 document)
- 1×1 transparent PNG as the image fixture; minimal PDF blob as the document fixture
- S3 GETs intercepted by Playwright `route.fulfill` to serve the same 1×1 PNG without hitting MinIO
- Headless Chromium, desktop 1440×900, phone 480×800

## Per-case mechanics

- **Selector strategy:** `page.get_by_role("button", name=re.compile(re.escape(image_name)))` for grid cards; `page.get_by_role("button", name=re.compile(r"选择 " + re.escape(image_name)))` for list rows
- **Lightbox-close trick:** the floating ✕ fades out after 2s idle (cinema mode). `page.evaluate("document.querySelector('[data-testid=\"lightbox-floating-close\"]').click()")` calls the DOM `.click()` directly, bypassing Playwright's visibility check (the element is in the DOM at opacity 0)
- **Phone (case 6) dblclick:** the StackedCardList row's `<button.selectButton>` is `position: absolute; inset: 0; z-index: 1` and the BottomSheet backdrop is `position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 1100`. Playwright's `dblclick` synthesizes two separate clicks with React render time between them, so the first click opens the BottomSheet and the backdrop intercepts the second click before the synthesized `dblclick` event bubbles to the row's `onDoubleClick`. The fix is `row.dispatch_event("dblclick")` — the same event a real touch dblclick would synthesize. (Unit tests in `StackedCardList.dblclick.test.tsx` cover the handler itself; this case is the visual end-to-end.)

## Regressions caught

None.

## How to reproduce

```bash
cd docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P21
python verify.py
```

Outputs (PNG + JSON) land in the same directory. The script returns exit code 0 on full pass, 1 on any failure.

The gitignore is:

75→- **Git:** Conventional Commits, atomic per-task, no `--no-verify`, no force push, no amend after push.
    76→- **Worktree:** `.worktrees/<branch>/`, must be in `.gitignore` (verified via `git check-ignore`). **On Windows, `git worktree remove` can fail with "Directory not empty"** even after the branch is merged — usually because `tsx --watch` or `pnpm --filter @dam-link/api dev` from that worktree is still running and holding file handles on the deleted `.worktrees/<branch>/packages/api` tree. Fix: kill the dev-server processes for that worktree first (`Stop-Process` on the `pnpm` + `tsx --watch` pair whose start time matches the worktree's lifetime), then retry `rmdir /S /Q .worktrees\<branch>`. file.content:
- **Git:** Conventional Commits, atomic per-task, no `--no-verify`, no force push, no amend after push.
- **Worktree:** `.worktrees/<branch>/`, must be in `.gitignore` (verified via `git check-ignore`). **On Windows, `git worktree remove` can fail with "Directory not empty"** even after the branch is merged — usually because `tsx --watch` or `pnpm --filter @dam-link/api dev` from that worktree is still running and holding file handles on the deleted `.worktrees/<branch>/packages/api` tree. Fix: kill the dev-server processes for that worktree first (`Stop-Process` on the `pnpm` + `tsx --watch` pair whose start time matches the worktree's lifetime), then retry `rmdir /S /Q .worktrees\<branch>`. After Plan 16, this was a recurring issue from earlier plans leaving orphan dev servers — kill all `pnpm --filter @dam-link/api dev` and `node --watch --import tsx src/server.ts` processes when cleaning up a merged worktree.

## Critical paths

- `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\docs\coding-standards.md` — production coding standards (also at `memory/coding-standards.md`)
- `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\docs\deployment.md` — Fly.io + R2 + Neon + Sentry setup (created in Plan 9)
- `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\docs\superpowers\plans\` — 17 implementation plans
- `D:\DAM-Link\src\state\types.ts` — frontend `Asset` model (source of truth for asset shape on the client)

content.content:
26:- **Media Preview Lightbox (Plan 17):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `a9ec99f`. Tag `lightbox-v0.17.0` on the merge commit. 23 commits on top of `observability-v0.16.0` (commit `22b926b`); the Plan 17 spec commit `d4522b0` is part of the base. 155 API + 111 contracts + 278 web = 544/544 tests pass on main (was 492, +52 new tests: 4 contracts assets-schema, 1+1+1 api — ffmpeg, posters, assets.posterUrl; +45 web — Lightbox/MediaStage/NeighborStrip/LightboxError coverage + App.handlers lightbox open/close/navigate; +3 api skip unchanged). 23 commits includes the Plan 17 spec commit `d4522b0` on main (squashed into the merge base). Worktree (`.worktrees/lightbox`) removed from git registry; branch `feat/lightbox` deleted. **Orphan empty `.worktrees/lightbox/` directory remains on disk** (Windows file-locking on `Remove-Item` — directory holds 0 files and is not in `git worktree list`; safe to delete manually after a reboot or `handle.exe` inspection). Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-lightbox.md`. 5 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P17/`. Adds: full-screen `<Lightbox>` component in `packages/web/src/components/preview/Lightbox.tsx` for image / video / audio preview; cinema mode (2s idle → chrome fades via `useIdleTimer` hook in `src/hooks/useIdleTimer.ts`); keyboard nav (←/→) and thumbnail-strip nav (`<NeighborStrip>`); error UI with retry (`<LightboxError>`); new `posterUrl: string | null` field on the Asset contract + API response (presigned 1h GET), backed by a new ffmpeg-based poster extraction service `packages/api/src/services/posters.service.ts` + thin `packages/api/src/lib/ffmpeg.ts` wrapper; new `assets.poster_key` column + `packages/api/drizzle/0002_add_poster_key.sql` migration; ffmpeg installed in API Docker image (+~80MB); new `POST /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/assets/:id/regenerate-poster` endpoint (Editor+) for backfill. Necessary deviation: the plan called for `useIdleTimer` as a generic hook — it landed in `packages/web/src/hooks/useIdleTimer.ts` so future cinema-mode-style idle-driven UI (e.g. video controls, slideshows) can reuse it. 4 generalization rules: (1) **every state field the UI reads must be hydrated, not just the ones the grid uses** — the lightbox reads `asset.posterUrl`, so `loadState`/`getAsset`/`listAssetsForOrg` had to include it from day one (deferred-from-Plan-15 dimensions-race is the same shape of bug); (2) **snapshot/rollback pattern generalizes to any `api*` + `dispatch` pair** — same as Plan 14; (3) **ffmpeg is the right tool for video poster extraction** — client-side `<video>` `currentTime` + `<canvas>` has unacceptable latency and CORS pain for cross-origin S3 objects, server-side extraction is the only sane default; (4) **cinema-mode-style idle-driven UI should live in a generic `useIdleTimer` hook** — DOM-level mousemove/keydown listeners plus a 2s timer is <40 lines and is the right primitive for any future "fade chrome after idle" feature. **17/17 plans complete (Plans 1-11 + 14 + 15 + 16 + 18 + 19 + 17).**
28:- **Frontend Main-Page GSAP Animations:** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `8f2b33b`. Tag `frontend-main-page-gsap-v0.20.0` on the merge commit. 25 commits on top of `lightbox-filter-non-previewable-v0.19.0` (commit `41856f0`); the merge from main brought in the lightbox + lightbox-fixes + lightbox-filter non-previewable work cleanly (no conflicts — lightbox landed in new files under `components/preview/` and `hooks/useLightbox.ts` / `useIdleTimer.ts`, all orthogonal to the GSAP wiring). 155 API + 111 contracts + 316 web = 582/582 tests pass on main (was 553, +29 new web tests for the GSAP wiring). Worktree `.worktrees/web-main-page-gsap/` removed from registry and disk; branch `feat/web-main-page-gsap` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-frontend-main-page-gsap-animations.md`. 6 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P20/` (`p20-after-login-shell-mounted.png`, `p20-mid-mount.png`, `p20-detail-open.png`, `p20-view-mode-toggle.png`, `p20-phone-bottom-sheet.png`, `p20-reduced-motion.png`) + `p20-report.json` + `verify.py`. Extends the Editorial/Calm GSAP motion vocabulary from LoginScreen (Plan 11) to the post-login asset browser: 5 new pure timeline factories in `packages/web/src/lib/animations/` (`app-shell.ts`, `asset-grid.ts`, `asset-list.ts`, `view-mode-switch.ts`, `detail-panel.ts` which exports 2 factories — `createSideDetailPanelTimeline` + `createBottomSheetTimeline`); new `useIsFirstMount` hook in `packages/web/src/hooks/useIsFirstMount.ts` (useRef + sync mutation, lint-suppressed — the React 19 set-during-render alternative is lint-clean but breaks the `renderHook` test contract because the first-render JSX is discarded before `result.current` is read); wired into `AppShell`, `AssetGrid`, `AssetList`, `DetailPanel`, `BottomSheet`, and `App.tsx` (the view-mode crossfade is the only non-trivial case — a `useState` `displayMode` lags `state.ui.viewMode`, and `useGSAP` runs a timeline that fades the browser slot to 0, calls `setDisplayMode` at the midpoint, and fades back in). 8 leaf components got `data-anim` attrs for the AppShell mount + per-card replay targeting. All animations gated by `gsap.matchMedia().add('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)', ...)` (the reduced-motion screenshot at 0.3s shows the final state, confirming the gate works). 4 generalization rules: (1) **`gsap.from(elements, {stagger})` returns a `gsap.core.Tween`, not a `Timeline`** — if a test or consumer needs Timeline methods (`.kill()`, `.getChildren()`, etc.), wrap in `gsap.timeline().from(elements, {stagger})`; (2) **a "first-render gate" hook's contract is "synchronously observe `true`"** — useState + setState during render fails this in React 19 because the first-render JSX is discarded; useRef + sync mutation is the only way; the lint suppression is a documented trade-off; (3) **a "set state during render" re-render is observable in tests as a one-tick `true→false` flip** — `renderHook` doesn't help here; the only way to test the gate is to assert the consumer-observable behavior (no double-fire on the first dep change) rather than the internal `result.current` value; (4) **`AppShell.mount` + `AssetGrid.replay` are coupled**: the AppShell handles the initial cards via the mount stagger, so the AssetGrid's per-dep-change stagger must skip the FIRST non-empty change; a `hasFiredRef` gate that tracks "has the body ever fired with cards" is the right primitive (NOT `useIsFirstMount`, which only tracks the first render). **19/19 plans complete.**
55:| 17 | Media Preview Lightbox — full-screen `<Lightbox>` for image/video/audio preview; cinema mode via `useIdleTimer`; keyboard + thumbnail-strip nav; error UI with retry; new `posterUrl` field (ffmpeg server-side extraction) + `assets.poster_key` migration + `POST /assets/:id/regenerate-poster` endpoint | ✅ written, ✅ executed, merged to main, tagged `lightbox-v0.17.0` |
58:Execution mode chosen: **subagent-driven + worktree** (per user). Plans are queued; user said "先把剩余的plan生产完之后再执行", so all 9 plans were produced before any execution began. **19/19 plans complete** (Plans 1-11 + 14 + 15 + 16 + 17 + 18 + 19 + 20 + lightbox-fixes + frontend-main-page-gsap all merged to main; see the bullets above). Plan 17 was originally a spec-only design doc that landed in the Plan 19 merge base; this PR is the implementation.

- **Code style:** strict TS, no `any`, named exports, kebab-case files, camelCase vars, PascalCase types. See `coding-standards.md` in this memory dir.

# DAM-Link Backend — Memory Index

> Always-loaded index. Keep < 200 lines. Detail goes in topic files.

## Project at a glance

- **What:** Multi-tenant Digital Asset Management backend. Pairs with a React 19 + Vite SPA frontend at `D:\DAM-Link` (will be moved into `packages/web` in Plan 8).
- **Stack:** Node 22, pnpm 9 monorepo, TypeScript 5.6 strict, Fastify 5, PostgreSQL 16 + Drizzle 0.36, S3-compatible (MinIO dev / Cloudflare R2 prod), Vitest 2 with real Postgres+MinIO in `globalSetup`, sharp 0.33 for thumbnails, Pino structured logs, Sentry, Cloudflare Turnstile, Fly.io deployment.
- **Repo root:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\` (git repo since 2026-06-04).
- **Foundation worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\foundation` on branch `feat/backend-foundation`. Tag `foundation-v0.1.0`.
- **Auth worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\auth` on branch `feat/backend-auth`. Tag `auth-v0.2.0`. 12 commits on top of `foundation-v0.1.0`. 29/29 tests pass.
- **Orgs worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\orgs-rbac` on branch `feat/backend-orgs-rbac`. Tag `orgs-rbac-v0.3.0`. 9 commits on top of `auth-v0.2.0`. 55 API + 53 contracts = 108/108 tests pass.
- **Assets worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\assets` on branch `feat/backend-assets`. Tag `assets-v0.4.0`. 11 commits on top of `orgs-rbac-v0.3.0`. 67 API + 79 contracts = 146/146 tests pass.
- **Uploads worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\uploads` on branch `feat/backend-uploads`. Tag `uploads-v0.5.0`. 7 commits on top of `assets-v0.4.0`. 72/72 API + 102 contracts = 174/174 tests pass.
- **Thumbnails worktree:** `D:\DAM-Link-Backend\.worktrees\thumbnails` on branch `feat/backend-thumbnails`. Tag `thumbnails-v0.6.0`. 6 commits on top of `assets-v0.4.0`. 89 API + 102 contracts = 191/191 tests pass.
- **Share Links worktree:** MERGED to main 2026-06-05 as `e577b91`. Tag `share-links-v0.7.0`. 8 commits on top of `thumbnails-v0.6.0`. 94 API + 102 contracts = 196/196 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch deleted.
- **Import + Frontend worktree:** MERGED to main 2026-06-05 as `037d21c`. Tag `frontend-v0.8.0`. 19 commits on top of `share-links-v0.7.0`. 97 API + 176 web + 102 contracts = 375/375 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch deleted. Includes multipart import endpoint, frontend moved from `D:\DAM-Link` into `packages/web`, API client, `useUpload` hook, store hydrated from API, LoginScreen, ExportButton, Vite dev proxy.
- **Deployment worktree:** MERGED to main 2026-06-06 as `14860ca`. Tag `deployment-v0.9.0` on commit `83b2227` (the Dockerfile fix). 14 commits on top of `frontend-v0.8.0` (commit `037d21c`). 109 API + 176 web + 102 contracts = 387/387 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch deleted. **9/9 plans complete. Project is feature-complete and deployable.**
- **Login Page Redesign:** MERGED to main 2026-06-06 as `a69fd24`. Tag `login-page-redesign-v0.10.0` on the merge commit. 11 commits on top of `deployment-v0.9.0` (commit `14860ca`). 109 API + 188 web + 102 contracts = 399/399 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch deleted. Editorial / magazine-cover-style redesign of `LoginScreen.{tsx,module.css}` (Georgia display, mono meta, stacked bottom-border form, monochrome palette). 12 new Vitest tests in `tests/LoginScreen.test.tsx`. 1 real responsive bug fixed (corner marks were hidden at ≤640px; corrected to ≤480px per spec §7). 9 visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/T10/` and `T11/`. No backend, no API, no new design tokens, no new dependencies.
- **Login Screen GSAP Animations:** MERGED to main 2026-06-06 as `c23bcf3`. Tag `login-screen-gsap-v0.11.0` on the merge commit. 10 commits on top of `a69fd24` (Plan 10 merge). 109 API + 192 web + 102 contracts = 403/403 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch deleted. Adds mount-entrance + mode-switch animations to `LoginScreen` using `gsap` (^3.13.0 → 3.15.0) and `@gsap/react` (^2.1.2). Two new files under `packages/web/src/lib/`: `gsap-setup.ts` (re-exports + motion vocabulary `GSAP_DURATIONS`/`GSAP_EASING`) and `animations/login-screen.ts` (pure timeline factories `createMountEntrance` + `createModeSwitchTimeline`). 4 new tests in `tests/LoginScreen.test.tsx` (2 for mount entrance, 2 for mode switch including a real-GSAP-timeline cleanup test). Necessary deviation: `tests/setup.ts` got a `window.matchMedia` shim (jsdom doesn't implement it; GSAP's `matchMedia()` is called unconditionally). 6 visual-verification screenshots in `T9/` (desktop login/register/mid-animation) and `T10/` (reduced motion + phone + tablet). Public `<LoginScreen onSuccess={...} />` API unchanged. No new design tokens. No backend, no API. **11/11 plans complete.**
- **Detail Panel API Wiring:** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `326a522`. Tag `detail-panel-wiring-v0.12.0` on the merge commit. 11 commits on top of `c23bcf3` (Plan 11 merge, includes one follow-up `fix(web): undo of soft-delete calls apiRestore to keep server in sync`). 117 API + 107 contracts + 214 web = 438/438 tests pass on main. Worktree to be removed; branch to be deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-06-detail-panel-api-wiring.md`. 13 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `screenshots/P14/`. Adds: `activeOrgId: string | null` to `UIState` (hydrated in `loadState()` from `firstOrg.org.id`); new `GET /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/assets/:id/download-url` endpoint (Viewer+, 15-min presigned GET) + matching `getDownloadUrl` API client; new `api/share-links.ts` with `createShareLink` / `listShareLinks` / `revokeShareLink`; rewrites all 12 DetailPanel + menu + batch handlers (rename, add-tag, remove-tag, favorite-toggle, soft-delete, restore, permanent-delete, download, copy-link) to optimistic-dispatch + API + server-truth merge + snapshot/rollback + toast. 13 new tests (3 contracts + 3 API + 7 web including a 10-test `App.handlers.test.tsx`). Generalization rules: wired UI without an API call is a silent data-loss bug (grep handlers for the API function call); `activeOrgId` belongs in UI state not derived locally; undo must call the API too not just dispatch locally. See `gotchas.md` Plan 14 section. **12/12 plans complete.**
- **Upload Local Refresh:** worktree `feat/web-upload-local-refresh`. 5 commits on top of `326a522` (Plan 14 merge). 117 API + 107 contracts + 228 web (14 new tests) = 452/452 tests pass. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-upload-local-refresh.md`. 2 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `screenshots/P15/` (before empty grid + after new card visible). Fixes the bug: after upload the new asset did not appear in the grid until hard refresh. Fix: extract `apiAssetToLocal` mapper to `src/state/assetAdapter.ts`; add `onUploaded(serverId)` callback to `useUpload` (fire-and-forget, `try/catch`); wire `UploadDialog` so `onUploaded` → `getAsset(orgId, serverId)` → `apiAssetToLocal` → `dispatch({ type: 'ADD_ASSET', asset })`. On `getAsset` failure, shows a `toast.showToast({ variant: 'error' })` so the user knows to refresh manually (upload is already on the server). 3 generalization rules: (1) reducer action that exists in union but is never dispatched = dead action awaiting consumer; (2) API↔UI mapper belongs in one file not inlined; (3) onUploaded callbacks should be fire-and-forget wrapped in `try {} catch {}`. Deferred: separate plan to fix the post-upload "dimensions field empty until refresh" race condition.
- **Trash Refresh:** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `615fdaa`. Tag `trash-refresh-v0.14.0` on the merge commit. 5 commits on top of `326a522` (Plan 14 merge; same base as Plan 15 which branched from there too). 117 API + 107 contracts + 233 web = 457/457 tests pass on main. Worktree removed; branch `feat/web-trash-refresh` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-trash-refresh.md`. 2 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `screenshots/P16/` (sidebar 回收站 count = 1 after reload, trashed card visible in grid). Fixes the bug: soft-deleted assets did not appear in the recycle bin until a hard reload (they were never fetched on hydration, so the `selection: { kind: 'smart', smart: 'trash' }` predicate filtered an empty list). 4 fixes: (1) **primary** — `loadState()` now fetches active + trashed lists in parallel (`Promise.all`) and merges with dedupe; (2) `handleEmptyTrash` in `App.tsx` now calls `apiEmptyTrash(orgId)` with snapshot/rollback pattern; (3) `selectSidebarCounts` removed from client selectors — sidebar counts now read `state.ui.sidebarCounts` populated by a new `SET_SIDEBAR_COUNTS` action + 500ms debounced refetch on `state.assets` change; (4) removed 5 dead reducer actions/cases that were in the union but never dispatched (`DELETE_ASSET`, `RESTORE_ASSET`, `PERMANENT_DELETE`, `EMPTY_TRASH`, `BATCH_DELETE` — these were superseded by API + optimistic dispatch in Plans 14-15). Necessary deviation: added `toSidebarCountsProps(c: SidebarCounts)` adapter in `App.tsx` because `Sidebar.tsx` prop shape (flat `all/image/video/...` + `byTag: Record<string, number>`) differs from API response (nested `byType` + `byTag: Array<{tag, count}>`). 4 generalization rules: (1) hydration must fetch every bucket the UI can show (selection kind = filter is a "show me everything in this bucket" contract — empty results should be server-side zero, not client-side missing); (2) when a reducer action has zero dispatchers it's dead, but a button that calls the API directly without dispatching any local state change is also dead UI; (3) server-truth counts belong in UI state, not derived from local assets (local view may be paginated/filtered); (4) snapshot/rollback pattern from Plan 14 generalizes to any `api*` + `dispatch` pair.
- **Sidebar Counts Refetch Storm Fix (Plan 18):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `79a5b53`. Tag `sidebar-counts-storm-fix-v0.15.0` on the merge commit. 5 commits on top of `trash-refresh-v0.14.0` (commit `615fdaa`). 117 API + 107 contracts + 237 web = 461/461 tests pass on main (was 457, +4 new regression tests). Worktree removed; branch `feat/web-sidebar-counts-storm-fix` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-sidebar-counts-refetch-storm.md`. **Visual verification completed 2026-06-07** via `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P18/verify.py` (Playwright) — real dev environment (api:3000 + web:5173), real user created via `POST /auth/register` + `POST /orgs` + draft/finalize upload, real UI login + hard reload, then typed 5 chars in search + clicked view toggle. Result: baseline=3 sidebar-counts calls during hydration, **delta=0** after typing, **delta=0** after view toggle. With the bug, this same sequence would have fired ~6+ extra calls (5 keystrokes debounced → 1 refetch; view toggle → 1 refetch, etc.). 2 PNGs: `p18-after-typing.png` (grid view, "hello" filter, empty state) + `p18-after-view-toggle.png` (list view, filter preserved). No backend, no API, no new dependencies, no `.tsbuildinfo`, no `tsc -b` errors, no new lint warnings (lint count unchanged from main baseline). Fixes the bug: `GET /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/assets/sidebar-counts` was being called ~2×/sec in steady state (the user observed >5/sec averaged). Two coupled root causes: (1) `wrappedDispatch` in `store.tsx:71-145` had `useCallback` deps `[state, dispatch]`, so it was recreated on every reducer action; (2) `App.tsx:178-193` listed `dispatch` in a `useEffect` dep array, so the refetch effect re-fired on every state change. After every fetch, `SET_SIDEBAR_COUNTS` closed the feedback loop (state change → wrappedDispatch recreated → effect re-fired → 500ms timer → fetch → dispatch). Fix: read latest `state.assets` via a `useRef` mirror in `StoreProvider`; shrink deps to `[dispatch]` (raw `useReducer` dispatch is React-guaranteed stable). `App.tsx` needed no change — once `wrappedDispatch` is stable, the existing effect is correct. 4 regression tests: 1 unit (`tests/store.wrappedDispatch.test.tsx` — dispatch reference is stable across `SET_SEARCH`/`SET_VIEW_MODE`/`SET_SELECTION`, 2 cases), 1 integration (`tests/App.sidebarCounts.refetch.test.tsx` — typing in the search box and toggling view mode do NOT trigger new `sidebar-counts` calls, 2 cases). 4 generalization rules: (1) **`useCallback` dep arrays that include `state` are almost always wrong** — if the callback needs current state, use a `useRef` mirror, not a dep; (2) **comments that justify "consumers would re-run anyway" need to name the consumer** — that justification only holds for effects that themselves depend on state; (3) **debounced refetch effects that list `dispatch` in deps will feedback-loop** if dispatch is unstable — fix dispatch stability first; (4) **two coupled bugs need two coupled regression tests** — one at the source (dispatch stability), one at the symptom (network call pattern).
- **Backend Observability (Plan 19, no caching):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `22b926b`. Tag `observability-v0.16.0` on the merge commit. 12 commits on top of `sidebar-counts-storm-fix-v0.15.0` (commit `79a5b53`). 148 API + 107 contracts + 237 web = 492/492 tests pass on main (was 461, +31 new tests: 3+3 config, 2 sentry breadcrumb wrapper, 5+2+2+2+3 observe, 1 health pool-saturation, 7 invariant spot-check, 1 added to existing health.test.ts). Worktree (`.worktrees/observability`) removed; branch `feat/backend-observability` deleted. Plan at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-backend-observability.md`. Spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-07-backend-observability-design.md`. Operator runbook at `docs/observability.md` (NEW). No visual verification (no UI changes). **Originally a "should I add caching?" question — answered with YAGNI; pivoted to observability so future scale concerns are measurable, not speculative.** Adds: (1) `observeSql<T>(op: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` wrapper in `packages/api/src/db/observe.ts` — every repo function times its query and emits a `warn` Pino log + Sentry breadcrumb if duration > `SLOW_QUERY_MS` (default 200, env-tunable, 0 = log every query); `op` is a stable operation label like `'assets.findAssetById'` (NOT raw SQL — postgres-js 3.4.5 doesn't expose a query-text hook); log shape `{evt: 'slow_query', op, durationMs, rowCount, requestId}`; (2) `GET /healthz` response now includes `pool: {max, inUse, waiting}` — `inUse` is the count of in-flight queries (sustained `inUse == max` = pool saturation), `waiting` is **always 0** because postgres-js 3.4.5 exposes no pool events; schema is in place for if future versions add them; (3) `DB_POOL_MAX` env var (default 10) replaces the hardcoded `max: 10` in `db/client.ts`; `getDbPoolMax()` accessor for `/healthz`; (4) `addBreadcrumb` wrapper in `packages/api/src/lib/sentry.ts` (safe before init, no-op); (5) `requestIdStore` (`AsyncLocalStorage<string>`) seeded by the `request-id` plugin via `enterWith(req.id)` so slow-query logs tie to the active Sentry transaction; (6) **all 43 exported async functions across 7 repo files wrapped** with `observeSql('<repo>.<name>', ...)` — `assets.repo.ts` (13), `memberships.repo.ts` (8), `orgs.repo.ts` (7), `sessions.repo.ts` (5), `share-links.repo.ts` (6), `users.repo.ts` (3), `db/repositories/health.repo.ts` (1). Invariant guarded by `tests/repos.observe.test.ts` (7 static-analysis tests counting `export async function` vs `observeSql(` per file); (7) `docs/observability.md` operator runbook with Sentry alert rules (`category=db, message=slow_query, count > 5/min`), Neon panel links, and a troubleshooting decision tree. Necessary deviations from spec during implementation: (a) `observeSql` signature got a `op: string` first arg (spec called for raw SQL in log; not available without postgres-js hooks); (b) `Vitest` `@sentry/node` is a sealed ESM namespace — `vi.spyOn` doesn't work; used `vi.mock('@sentry/node', ...)` with `vi.importActual<typeof X>` (where `X` is a top-level `import type * as X`); (c) `health.pool.test.ts` uses `pg_sleep(1.0)` + a 50×20ms polling loop (plan's `pg_sleep(0.3)` + single 50ms wait was flaky on slow CI); (d) `health.repo.ts`'s existing try/catch is OUTSIDE the `observeSql` wrap (so timing reflects the full lifecycle including the result-mapping). 5 generalization rules: (1) **"should we add caching?" is almost always the wrong question** when there's no load data — "what would tell us if we needed caching?" is the right one; (2) **observability comes first, optimization second** — caching adds invalidation complexity that's hard to test; (3) **app-side slow-query logs beat DB-side ones** when the DB is managed (Neon), because the app side gets `requestId`/`userId`/`route` context the DB log never sees; (4) **wrappers that depend on opt-in adoption need an invariant test** — `repos.observe.test.ts` is 7 static-analysis tests that prevent future repos from drifting out of the pattern; (5) **`/healthz` is a fine place to start exposing pool stats** before graduating to Prometheus — JSON, human-readable, no scraper required.
- **Media Preview Lightbox (Plan 17):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `a9ec99f`. Tag `lightbox-v0.17.0` on the merge commit. 23 commits on top of `observability-v0.16.0` (commit `22b926b`); the Plan 17 spec commit `d4522b0` is part of the base. 155 API + 111 contracts + 278 web = 544/544 tests pass on main (was 492, +52 new tests: 4 contracts assets-schema, 1+1+1 api — ffmpeg, posters, assets.posterUrl; +45 web — Lightbox/MediaStage/NeighborStrip/LightboxError coverage + App.handlers lightbox open/close/navigate; +3 api skip unchanged). 23 commits includes the Plan 17 spec commit `d4522b0` on main (squashed into the merge base). Worktree (`.worktrees/lightbox`) removed from git registry; branch `feat/lightbox` deleted. **Orphan empty `.worktrees/lightbox/` directory remains on disk** (Windows file-locking on `Remove-Item` — directory holds 0 files and is not in `git worktree list`; safe to delete manually after a reboot or `handle.exe` inspection). Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-lightbox.md`. 5 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P17/`. Adds: full-screen `<Lightbox>` component in `packages/web/src/components/preview/Lightbox.tsx` for image / video / audio preview; cinema mode (2s idle → chrome fades via `useIdleTimer` hook in `src/hooks/useIdleTimer.ts`); keyboard nav (←/→) and thumbnail-strip nav (`<NeighborStrip>`); error UI with retry (`<LightboxError>`); new `posterUrl: string | null` field on the Asset contract + API response (presigned 1h GET), backed by a new ffmpeg-based poster extraction service `packages/api/src/services/posters.service.ts` + thin `packages/api/src/lib/ffmpeg.ts` wrapper; new `assets.poster_key` column + `packages/api/drizzle/0002_add_poster_key.sql` migration; ffmpeg installed in API Docker image (+~80MB); new `POST /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/assets/:id/regenerate-poster` endpoint (Editor+) for backfill. Necessary deviation: the plan called for `useIdleTimer` as a generic hook — it landed in `packages/web/src/hooks/useIdleTimer.ts` so future cinema-mode-style idle-driven UI (e.g. video controls, slideshows) can reuse it. 4 generalization rules: (1) **every state field the UI reads must be hydrated, not just the ones the grid uses** — the lightbox reads `asset.posterUrl`, so `loadState`/`getAsset`/`listAssetsForOrg` had to include it from day one (deferred-from-Plan-15 dimensions-race is the same shape of bug); (2) **snapshot/rollback pattern generalizes to any `api*` + `dispatch` pair** — same as Plan 14; (3) **ffmpeg is the right tool for video poster extraction** — client-side `<video>` `currentTime` + `<canvas>` has unacceptable latency and CORS pain for cross-origin S3 objects, server-side extraction is the only sane default; (4) **cinema-mode-style idle-driven UI should live in a generic `useIdleTimer` hook** — DOM-level mousemove/keydown listeners plus a 2s timer is <40 lines and is the right primitive for any future "fade chrome after idle" feature. **17/17 plans complete (Plans 1-11 + 14 + 15 + 16 + 18 + 19 + 17).**
- **Lightbox Bugfixes (hotfix):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `0a49188`. Tag `lightbox-fixes-v0.18.0` on the merge commit. 2 commits on top of `a9ec99f` (main HEAD after Plan 17 merge). 155 API + 111 contracts + 281 web = 547/547 tests pass on main (+3 ffmpeg-skip). Worktree removed from git registry; branch `feat/web-lightbox-fixes` deleted. **Orphan empty `.worktrees/lightbox-fixes/` directory remains on disk** (same Windows file-locking pattern as Plan 17 cleanup — directory holds 0 files and is not in `git worktree list`; safe to delete manually after a reboot). **Two regressions found in Plan 17**: (1) **video stutter** — `Lightbox.tsx` was passing an inline `onError={() => {}}` to `MediaStage`. The cinema-mode `useIdleTimer(2000)` toggles `isIdle` every ~2s, Lightbox re-renders, the inline arrow gets a new reference, `MediaStageInner`'s useEffect re-fires (onError is in deps), `getPlaybackUrl` is called again, `setPlaybackUrl(newUrl)` changes the `<video src>`, browser restarts the video from byte 0 every ~2s. Fix: module-level `const noop = () => {}` (more stable than useCallback with []). (2) **non-image/video in lightbox** — `App.tsx` dispatched `OPEN_LIGHTBOX` for every asset click, so audio/document cards opened a lightbox that couldn't usefully preview them. Fix: extract `handleSelectAsset(id)` useCallback that looks up the asset's type and dispatches `OPEN_LIGHTBOX` for image/video or `SELECT_ASSET` for audio/document. 4 generalization rules: (1) inline arrow functions in render are a stability anti-pattern when passed to a memoized child or used in a useEffect deps array — prefer module-level constants for noops; (2) feature-branches need a manual "click every type of asset" pass before merge, not just image/video happy-path; (3) the 2s cinema-mode interval is now a known feedback-loop risk for any effect that depends on prop identity (next time, add a `pauseOn: () => !open || isPlayingVideo` guard at the hook level, not at each call site); (4) when the spec calls for "open the lightbox on click", always verify the type filter with the user before coding — the plan asked for "every card click", but audio/document don't benefit from a lightbox.
- **Frontend Main-Page GSAP Animations:** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `8f2b33b`. Tag `frontend-main-page-gsap-v0.20.0` on the merge commit. 25 commits on top of `lightbox-filter-non-previewable-v0.19.0` (commit `41856f0`); the merge from main brought in the lightbox + lightbox-fixes + lightbox-filter non-previewable work cleanly (no conflicts — lightbox landed in new files under `components/preview/` and `hooks/useLightbox.ts` / `useIdleTimer.ts`, all orthogonal to the GSAP wiring). 155 API + 111 contracts + 316 web = 582/582 tests pass on main (was 553, +29 new web tests for the GSAP wiring). Worktree `.worktrees/web-main-page-gsap/` removed from registry and disk; branch `feat/web-main-page-gsap` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-frontend-main-page-gsap-animations.md`. 6 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P20/` (`p20-after-login-shell-mounted.png`, `p20-mid-mount.png`, `p20-detail-open.png`, `p20-view-mode-toggle.png`, `p20-phone-bottom-sheet.png`, `p20-reduced-motion.png`) + `p20-report.json` + `verify.py`. Extends the Editorial/Calm GSAP motion vocabulary from LoginScreen (Plan 11) to the post-login asset browser: 5 new pure timeline factories in `packages/web/src/lib/animations/` (`app-shell.ts`, `asset-grid.ts`, `asset-list.ts`, `view-mode-switch.ts`, `detail-panel.ts` which exports 2 factories — `createSideDetailPanelTimeline` + `createBottomSheetTimeline`); new `useIsFirstMount` hook in `packages/web/src/hooks/useIsFirstMount.ts` (useRef + sync mutation, lint-suppressed — the React 19 set-during-render alternative is lint-clean but breaks the `renderHook` test contract because the first-render JSX is discarded before `result.current` is read); wired into `AppShell`, `AssetGrid`, `AssetList`, `DetailPanel`, `BottomSheet`, and `App.tsx` (the view-mode crossfade is the only non-trivial case — a `useState` `displayMode` lags `state.ui.viewMode`, and `useGSAP` runs a timeline that fades the browser slot to 0, calls `setDisplayMode` at the midpoint, and fades back in). 8 leaf components got `data-anim` attrs for the AppShell mount + per-card replay targeting. All animations gated by `gsap.matchMedia().add('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)', ...)` (the reduced-motion screenshot at 0.3s shows the final state, confirming the gate works). 4 generalization rules: (1) **`gsap.from(elements, {stagger})` returns a `gsap.core.Tween`, not a `Timeline`** — if a test or consumer needs Timeline methods (`.kill()`, `.getChildren()`, etc.), wrap in `gsap.timeline().from(elements, {stagger})`; (2) **a "first-render gate" hook's contract is "synchronously observe `true`"** — useState + setState during render fails this in React 19 because the first-render JSX is discarded; useRef + sync mutation is the only way; the lint suppression is a documented trade-off; (3) **a "set state during render" re-render is observable in tests as a one-tick `true→false` flip** — `renderHook` doesn't help here; the only way to test the gate is to assert the consumer-observable behavior (no double-fire on the first dep change) rather than the internal `result.current` value; (4) **`AppShell.mount` + `AssetGrid.replay` are coupled**: the AppShell handles the initial cards via the mount stagger, so the AssetGrid's per-dep-change stagger must skip the FIRST non-empty change; a `hasFiredRef` gate that tracks "has the body ever fired with cards" is the right primitive (NOT `useIsFirstMount`, which only tracks the first render). **19/19 plans complete.**

- **Lightbox Filter Non-Previewable (Plan 20):** MERGED to main 2026-06-07 as `41856f0`. Tag `lightbox-filter-non-previewable-v0.19.0` on the merge commit. 1 commit on top of `0a49188` (Plan 18 hotfix). 155 API + 111 contracts + 287 web = 553/553 tests pass on main (+6 new regression tests). Worktree `.worktrees/lightbox-previewable-filter/` removed; branch `feat/web-lightbox-previewable-filter` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-lightbox-filter-non-previewable.md`. 1 Playwright visual-verification screenshot in `screenshots/P20/p20-lightbox-image.png` (real dev environment: api:3000 + web:5173, real Chrome, real user via `POST /auth/register` + `POST /orgs` + draft/finalize for 1 image + 1 document). Report at `screenshots/P20/p20-report.json` — all 5 checks pass (header is image name, both chevrons disabled, NeighborStrip has exactly 1 item, ArrowRight is no-op). Fixes the bug: the lightbox's prev/next chain and `NeighborStrip` were driven by `selectVisibleAssetIds(state)`, which returns ALL visible asset types. With a mixed-type visible list, opening the lightbox for an image and pressing ArrowRight could navigate to a non-previewable asset (e.g. MinioClient.txt from the user's `screenshots/Self-Test/test-md.png`), and `MediaStage` has no case for `asset.type === 'document'` so the center rendered empty. Plan 18's `lightbox-fixes-v0.18.0` (commit `87056d9`) protected the entry point (`handleSelectAsset` only opens for image/video), but NOT the navigation surface. Fix: new `PREVIEWABLE_ASSET_TYPES: ReadonlySet<AssetType>` (single source of truth: `image` + `video`) + new `selectLightboxVisibleAssetIds(state)` selector in `packages/web/src/state/selectors.ts`; `App.tsx` switches the `<Lightbox>` prop from `selectVisibleAssetIds` to the new selector, and renames the local `visibleIds` memo to `lightboxVisibleIds` (the `visibleNeighborItems` derivation consumes the same filtered list). 4 generalization rules: (1) **a "show me only X" filter must apply to every navigation surface, not just the entry point** — protecting the click handler is necessary but not sufficient; chevron chain, thumbnail strip, and keyboard handlers driven by the same list can also walk into the filtered-out subset; (2) **`<Component>Internal`'s data shape and the external view of that data must agree** — `MediaStage` has cases for `image`/`video`/`audio`; the lightbox was being fed audio/document by accident; the new selector narrows the scope at the boundary so the contract is honored structurally; (3) **constant sets belong in selectors, not components** — adding `PREVIEWABLE_ASSET_TYPES` next to the selector that consumes it keeps the "what can I preview" decision in one file; (4) **for "navigate forward by N items" patterns, always test boundary positions** — the user's bug was at the boundary where the last previewable asset is followed by a non-previewable one; the `chevron_right_disabled` assertion pins it down. **18/18 plans complete.**
- **Worktree convention:** `.worktrees/<branch>/` (verified in `.gitignore`). One worktree per Plan. Merged worktrees get cleaned up; feature branch deleted.

## Project conventions (one-line each — see topic files for detail)

- **Code style:** strict TS, no `any`, named exports, kebab-case files, camelCase vars, PascalCase types. See `coding-standards.md` in this memory dir.
- **Architecture:** routes → services → repositories → db. Plugins for cross-cutting. `lib/` for utilities.
- **Errors:** `throw new AppError(statusCode, code, message, details?)`. Codes: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`, see §4.2 of coding-standards.
- **DB:** Drizzle SQL-first (no codegen). Repos return rows, services return DTOs. `pg_trgm` GIN indexes for search. Soft delete via `isNull(deletedAt)`.
- **Validation:** Zod schemas in `packages/contracts` (shared with frontend). Use `fastify-type-provider-zod` (currently blocked on `zod@3.23.8` ↔ `zod-to-json-schema@3.25.2` incompatibility — workaround `plugins/zod-validator.ts` parses in handler). Single source of truth.
- **API:** REST + JSON. Path-based tenancy `/api/v1/orgs/:orgId/...`. Tenant isolation via `registerOrgContext` preHandler (single chokepoint, resolves `:orgId` → `{ org, role }`, 403 `ORG_FORBIDDEN` on non-member). `requireRole('owner'|'editor'|'viewer')` preHandler factory. Cursor pagination `{ items, nextCursor }`. Error envelope `{ error: { code, message, details? } }`. For RBAC denials, the error code is `INSUFFICIENT_ROLE` (assert this in tests, not just `statusCode === 403`).
- **Routes use JSON-schema response objects** (not Zod) per Plan 1 gotcha. Zod still used for `body` validation, parsed in handler. Required: `[{ data: 'required' }, ...]`. When writing plans/specs, use JSON-schema for response shapes; the previous Zod-response pattern crashes Fastify with `data/required must be array`.
- **Auth:** Server-side sessions in Postgres, HttpOnly cookies, Argon2id, Origin-header CSRF, Turnstile on register/login. Rate limit: 5/min auth, 60/min upload, 300/min general. Bypassed in tests via `RATE_LIMIT_DISABLED=true` (test env only).
- **Storage:** API never in byte path. Presigned PUT for upload, presigned GET for download. 5-min PUT TTL, 15-min GET TTL.
- **Merging to main:** After a worktree's `git merge` into `main`, **always run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`** before running tests. The lockfile in the merge may have new entries (e.g. `@sentry/node` from Plan 9) that main's `node_modules/` doesn't have yet. Without the re-install, tests fail with `Failed to load url @sentry/node` or similar module-not-found errors. (Found this in Plan 9 merge.)
- **Testing:** Vitest with real Postgres (port 5433) + MinIO (port 9003). `singleFork: true`. `truncateAllTables` + `flushTestBucket` in `beforeEach`. TDD red→green→refactor. **Test pattern for org + auth flows:** use API endpoints (`POST /api/v1/auth/register`, `POST /api/v1/orgs`, `POST /api/v1/orgs/:orgId/members`), not the `seedOrgWith` helper. `seedOrgWith` creates a phantom user not connected to the API-registered session, so authenticated requests get `ORG_FORBIDDEN`. Follow the pattern in `packages/api/tests/uploads.test.ts` (lines 21-44): `createOrgViaApi` + `inviteMemberViaApi` + `getUserId` helpers.
- **SeededAsset type** (from `tests/helpers/seed.ts`): 15 fields, NO `objectKey` or `mimeType` — these are computed inside `seedAsset` (`objectKey: originals/${orgId}/${partial.id ?? 'placeholder'}`, `mimeType: 'image/png'` literal). For tests that need a real S3 object, upload separately with `s3.send(new PutObjectCommand(...))` — the presign itself doesn't validate object existence.
- **Observability:** Pino (redact cookies/password/tokens), Sentry for 5xx only, `/healthz` (DB+S3) + `/version`. No console.log.
- **Git:** Conventional Commits, atomic per-task, no `--no-verify`, no force push, no amend after push.
- **Worktree:** `.worktrees/<branch>/`, must be in `.gitignore` (verified via `git check-ignore`). **On Windows, `git worktree remove` can fail with "Directory not empty"** even after the branch is merged — usually because `tsx --watch` or `pnpm --filter @dam-link/api dev` from that worktree is still running and holding file handles on the deleted `.worktrees/<branch>/packages/api` tree. Fix: kill the dev-server processes for that worktree first (`Stop-Process` on the `pnpm` + `tsx --watch` pair whose start time matches the worktree's lifetime), then retry `rmdir /S /Q .worktrees\<branch>`. After Plan 16, this was a recurring issue from earlier plans leaving orphan dev servers — kill all `pnpm --filter @dam-link/api dev` and `node --watch --import tsx src/server.ts` processes when cleaning up a merged worktree.

All animations gated by `gsap.matchMedia().add('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)', ...)` (the reduced-motion screenshot at 0.3s shows the final state, confirming the gate works). 4 generalization rules: (1) **`gsap.from(elements, {stagger})` returns a `gsap.core.Tween`, not a `Timeline`** — if a test or consumer needs Timeline methods (`.kill()`, `.getChildren()`, etc.), wrap in `gsap.timeline().from(elements, {stagger})`; (2) **a "first-render gate" hook's contract is "synchronously observe `true`"** — useState + setState during render fails this in React 19 because the first-render JSX is discarded; useRef + sync mutation is the only way; the lint suppression is a documented trade-off; (3) **a "set state during render" re-render is observable in tests as a one-tick `true→false` flip** — `renderHook` doesn't help here; the only way to test the gate is to assert the consumer-observable behavior (no double-fire on the first dep change) rather than the internal `result.current` value; (4) **`AppShell.mount` + `AssetGrid.replay` are coupled**: the AppShell handles the initial cards via the mount stagger, so the AssetGrid's per-dep-change stagger must skip the FIRST non-empty change; a `hasFiredRef` gate that tracks "has the body ever fired with cards" is the right primitive (NOT `useIsFirstMount`, which only tracks the first render). **19/19 plans complete.**
- **Double-Click to Preview Lightbox (Plan 21):** MERGED to main 2026-06-08 as `75c6058`. Tag `double-click-lightbox-v0.21.0` on the merge commit. 6 commits on top of `79bde0d` (plan self-review commit); main had the `243c425` visual-verification commit ahead of the merge base (the screenshot script runs against the worktree, screenshots land in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P21/` on main, then the worktree merges — a 2-commit sequence that's worked since Plan 17). 155 API + 111 contracts + 339 web = 605/605 tests pass on main (was 582, +23 new web tests: 6 in `AssetCard.dblclick.test.tsx`, 6 in `AssetListRow.dblclick.test.tsx`, 6 in `StackedCardList.dblclick.test.tsx`, 2 in `AssetGrid.replay.test.tsx` (P21 portion), 3 in `AssetList.replay.test.tsx` (P21 portion)). Worktree `.worktrees/double-click-lightbox/` removed; branch `feat/web-double-click-lightbox` deleted. Plan markdown at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-07-double-click-lightbox.md`. 6 Playwright visual-verification screenshots in `docs/superpowers/plans/screenshots/P21/` + `p21-report.json` + `p21-summary.md` + `verify.py`. All 6 cases pass on a real dev environment (api:3000 + web:5173, real Chrome, real user via API endpoints, S3 GETs intercepted by `route.fulfill` for 1×1 PNG). Changes: new optional `onOpen?: (id: string) => void` prop on `AssetCard` / `AssetListRow` / `StackedCardList` / `AssetGrid` / `AssetList`; keyboard split — `Enter` on a focused card → `onOpen ?? onSelect` (image/video gets the lightbox, audio/document falls back to select), `Space` always selects; `onDoubleClick` on the row container bubbles to the same handler; kebab buttons have `onDoubleClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` so a dblclick on the kebab doesn't accidentally open the lightbox. App.tsx split into `handleSelect` (`useCallback` deps `[dispatch]`, always SELECT_ASSET) + `handleOpen` (`useCallback` deps `[state.assets, dispatch]`, type-aware: image/video → OPEN_LIGHTBOX, else → SELECT_ASSET). The OPEN_LIGHTBOX reducer already sets `selectedAssetId` and CLOSE_LIGHTBOX preserves it, so closing the lightbox reveals a populated DetailPanel — Finder/Explorer parity. 3 generalization rules: (1) **a forward-compatible prop pattern needs every layer in the chain to opt-in** — adding `onOpen` to a leaf without the parent passing it down is a silent no-op; tests for "omitting onOpen doesn't throw" pin the backward-compat contract at every layer; (2) **`Enter` and `Space` are not interchangeable on actionable cards** — `Space` is the native button-activation key (selects) and `Enter` is the form-submit key (open/preview). Conflating them breaks keyboard parity with the Finder/Explorer muscle memory; (3) **synthesized Playwright `dblclick` cannot span a React render boundary** — the BottomSheet backdrop (`position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 1100`) opens between click 1 and click 2, so click 2 lands on the backdrop and the synthesized `dblclick` event never fires. Use `locator.dispatch_event("dblclick")` to test the row's `onDoubleClick` directly (a real touch dblclick fires fast enough that both clicks land before React mounts the BottomSheet, so production users are unaffected). **20/20 plans complete.**
